sections.heading: TNM
sections.content: {"system": "AJCC Cancer Staging Manual, 8th Edition (2017)", "t_stages": [{"stage": "TX", "description": "Primary tumor cannot be assessed."}, {"stage": "T0", "description": "No evidence of primary tumor."}, {"stage": "Tis", "description": "Carcinoma in situ; high-grade dysplasia/intraepithelial neoplasia."}, {"stage": "T1", "description": "Tumor invades the lamina propria, muscularis mucosae, or submucosa."}, {"stage": "T1a", "description": "Tumor invades the lamina propria or muscularis mucosae."}, {"stage": "T1b", "description": "Tumor invades the submucosa."}, {"stage": "T2", "description": "Tumor invades the muscularis propria."}, {"stage": "T3", "description": "Tumor invades the adventitia."}, {"stage": "T4", "description": "Tumor invades adjacent structures."}, {"stage": "T4a", "description": "Tumor invades the pleura, pericardium, azygos vein, diaphragm, or peritoneum."}, {"stage": "T4b", "description": "Tumor invades other adjacent structures, such as the aorta, vertebral body, or airway."}], "n_stages": [{"stage": "NX", "description": "Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed."}, {"stage": "N0", "description": "No regional lymph node metastasis."}, {"stage": "N1", "description": "Metastasis in 1-2 regional lymph nodes."}, {"stage": "N2", "description": "Metastasis in 3-6 regional lymph nodes."}, {"stage": "N3", "description": "Metastasis in 7 or more regional lymph nodes."}], "m_stages": [{"stage": "M0", "description": "No distant metastasis."}, {"stage": "M1", "description": "Distant metastasis."}], "stage_groups": [{"group": "Stage 0", "criteria": "Tis, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "High-grade dysplasia/carcinoma in situ, confined to epithelium.", "five_yr_survival": "Nearly 100% with appropriate treatment (endoscopic or surgical).", "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage I", "criteria": "Varies by histology, but generally includes T1a-T1b, N0, M0 (low-risk)", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor limited to the mucosa or submucosa without nodal involvement.", "five_yr_survival": ">85% for T1a; ~70% for T1b depending on risk factors.", "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage II", "criteria": "Varies by histology, includes T2-T3, N0, M0; or T1-T2, N1, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor invades deeper muscle layers or has limited nodal spread.", "five_yr_survival": "30-60% depending on exact stage.", "treatment_intent": "Curative"}, {"group": "Stage III", "criteria": "Varies by histology, includes T3-T4a, N1-N2, M0; or T1-T3, N2-N3, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Locally advanced disease with significant nodal involvement or invasion into adventitia/adjacent structures (resectable).", "five_yr_survival": "10-30%.", "treatment_intent": "Curative with multimodal therapy"}, {"group": "Stage IVA", "criteria": "T4b (any N, M0) OR any T, N3, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Unresectable local disease due to invasion of critical structures (T4b) or bulky nodal disease (N3).", "five_yr_survival": "<5%.", "treatment_intent": "Palliative; systemic therapy and/or definitive chemoradiation"}, {"group": "Stage IVB", "criteria": "Any T, Any N, M1", "clinical_meaning": "Distant metastatic disease.", "five_yr_survival": "<2%.", "treatment_intent": "Palliative; systemic therapy and best supportive care"}], "staging_pearls": ["The AJCC 8th edition uses separate stage groupings for clinical (cTNM), pathologic (pTNM), and post-neoadjuvant therapy (ypTNM) staging.", "For esophageal SCC, tumor location (upper, middle, lower) influences stage groupings.", "For adenocarcinoma of the EGJ, the Siewert classification helps guide surgical approach: Type I (epicenter 1-5 cm above EGJ) and II (epicenter 1 cm above to 2 cm below EGJ) are staged as esophageal cancer; Type III (epicenter 2-5 cm below EGJ) is staged as gastric cancer.", "Celiac lymph node involvement in EGJ/distal esophageal cancers should be considered for combined modality therapy.", "The number of lymph nodes examined is critical: at least 16 nodes should be removed for adequate staging in surgery without neoadjuvant therapy."]}

t_stages.description: Carcinoma in situ; high-grade dysplasia/intraepithelial neoplasia.
Tumor invades the lamina propria, muscularis mucosae, or submucosa.
Tumor invades the lamina propria or muscularis mucosae.
Tumor invades the pleura, pericardium, azygos vein, diaphragm, or peritoneum.
Tumor invades other adjacent structures, such as the aorta, vertebral body, or airway.

n_stages.description: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed.
Metastasis in 7 or more regional lymph nodes.
